description.en: A type of comparison between two or more resources.
A group of task or runs to easily refer to them and share them with others.
The architecture that represents the set of hardware, software, network resources and services required for the operation of an IT environment.
A measurable property of datasets, such as size, shape, statistical properties, benchmarks, and the presence of missing values.
The measurable effects of activities on the environment.
How models should be evaluated, typically by defining specific kinds of train- and test splits.
A way to score the outputs of machine learning models (e.g. predictions).
The attribute or column in which a distribution is structured.
A physical embodiment of the machine learning model in a particular format.
A machine learning pipeline or neural architectures, or (untrained) machine learning models, such as in general.
The tangible, physical devices and components that support the operation of software applications and services.
The combination of the probability of an occurrence of harm and the severity of that harm.
A collection of code.
A file that has been trained to recognize certain types of patterns.
The distinct format of structure of input or output data.
The degree or extent of something, as determined by measurement or calculation.
The various configuration settings that are specified prior to and during training a model.
An evaluation of machine learning models (flows) trained on a given task.
A group of runs to easily refer to them and share them with others.
Represents a partition of a distribution of data.
A task defines specific problems to be solved using a given dataset.
A group of tasks to easily refer to them and share them with others.
Settings for a dataset that determine who can access or view it. Options typically include 'Everyone', 'All my friends', 'Only me', or specific user-defined circles.
Settings for a dataset that determine who can access or view it. Options typically include 'Everyone', 'All my friends', 'Only me', or specific user-defined circles.
User-provided version label, which can be a date, hash, or other identifier relevant to the user.
The version of the flow.
Version of the resource.
The result of evaluation.
The url of the file to be downloaded.
The time that the flow was uploaded.

A description of any methods implemented in data acquisition or processing, if any, to detect the presence of unsuitable data sources considering the model's intended uses, including but not limited to illegal content, child sexual abuse material (CSAM), non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII), and personal data leading to its unlawful processing.
A description of the training time.
A description of the training process.
A description of the methodologies used.
The total number of parameters of the model.

A detailed description of the measures put in place for the purpose of conducting internal and/or external adversarial testing (e.g. red teaming), model adaptations, including alignment and fine-tuning.
The origin or cause of the risk.
The maximum size of the modality.
A short description of a machine learning model.
The extent of the damage resulting from an event occurring.
The attribute that represents the row-id column, if present in the dataset.
The number of the repeat in the outer cross-validation.
The date a model is released.
Warnings while processing the dataset.
Errors discovered while processing the dataset.
Date of processing.
The likelihood that a given event will occur.
The consumed power.
Date when the model was placed on the Union market.
The total count of individual data entries or observations contained.
The inherent characteristics of the risk.
A general description of the model architecture, e.g. a transformer architecture.
The action of reducing the likelihood or severity of the risk.
A method of distribution through which the model has been made or can be made available to downstream providers.
The methodology with which the resource was measured.
The limited capabilities of the model.
The number of likes the machine learning model received.
The minimum value of the Quality Measure.
The maximum value of the Quality Measure.
The prescribed application of the model.
Attributes that should be excluded in modelling, such as identifiers and indexes.
An explanation of how to use or deploy the model.
The url or location of the document itself.
The number of the fold in the inner cross-validation.
The version of the flow, as defined by the uploader, for reference.
A description of the evaluation strategies used.
A description of the evaluation against the validation dataset.
The engine responsible for extracting the features.
The number of downloads of the machine learning model.

A general description of the main steps or stages involved in the training process, including training methodologies and techniques, the key design choices, assumptions made and what the model is designed to optimise for, and the relevance of different parameters, as applicable.
Version number of the description. '1' for original version.
The default value of the parameter.
The primary attribute intended for analysis or processing, if applicable.
A documented trail that accounts for the origin of the data.

General description of the data processing involved in transforming the acquired data into training, testing, and validation data for the model, such as cleaning (e.g. filtering out irrelevant content such as advertisements), normalisation (e.g. tokenizing), augmentation (e.g. back-translation).
Date of creation of the resource.
Method used to collect the data, provided by the uploader.
The date the data was originally collected, given by the uploader.
The classification of the modality.
The emitted carbon.
A description of any methods implemented in data acquisition or processing, if any, to address the prevalence of identifiable biases in the training data.
A description of the possible biases affecting the overall output of the model.
For composite evaluation measures (e.g. per-class measures, confusion matrix), a string (JSON) representation of the evaluation.
A dataset of quality measurements, evaluations of one or more datasets (or dataset distributions) against specific quality metrics.
The policy expressing the rights associated with the distribution if using the [[ODRL]] vocabulary.
The current version of the model.
A keyword or tag describing the resource.
A tag describing the resource.
The name assigned to the resource.
An unambiguous identifier for the resource.
A classification of the resource.
A classification of the resource.
The data type of the parameter.
A classification of the resource.
The nature or genre of the resource.

The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

The library that is executed by this file.
The model that is variant of this model.
Measured or estimated amount of energy used for training.
The split that is used for the estimation procedure.
A possible risk that can occur.
The configured setting.
The configured setting.
The modality of the output of the model.
The library associated with the computer infrastructure.
The modality of the input of the model.
The impact of inferencing the model.
The hardware associated with the computer infrastructure.
The reference to the flow used in this run.
The file in which the model is expressed.
Statistics resulting of user engagement.
The libraries that a flow depends on.
The benchmarks used to evaluate this model.
The model from which this model has been fine tuned.
